Class Tower

java.lang.Object
Tower
public class Tower extends Object
Main controller class for the stacking items tower simulator. Manages a tower where cups and lids can be stacked vertically. Inspired by the ICPC 2025 Problem J "Stacking Cups".
Version:
2.0
Author:
Acero - Quiceno

    Methods inherited from class java.lang.Object

    clone, equals, getClass, hashCode, notify, notifyAll, toString, wait, wait, wait

  • Constructor Details

    • Tower

      public Tower(int width, int maxHeight)
      Create a new Tower with the given dimensions. The tower starts empty with height 0.
      Parameters:
      width - the logical width of the tower (max cup diameter that fits)
      maxHeight - the maximum stacking height in cm

  • Method Details

    • pushCup

      public void pushCup(int i)
      Push a new cup onto the top of the tower. Creates a cup with the given id. The diameter equals the id (logical), the height is calculated as (2*id - 1) cm, and the color is assigned automatically from a cyclic palette. Can occur somethings, so, this changes tower status (isOk = false) When:
      • id is not positive
      • a cup with the same id already exists
      • the cup's diameter exceeds the tower width
      • the cup's height would exceed the remaining tower capacity
      Parameters:
      i - the unique id of the cup to create and push

    • pushLid

      public void pushLid(int i)
      Push a new lid onto the top of the tower. Creates a lid with the given id. If a cup with the same id exists, the lid inherits its color and width; otherwise defaults are used. Lids are always 1 cm tall. Can occur somethings, so, this changes tower status (isOk = false) When:
      • id is not positive
      • a lid with the same id already exists
      • the lid's width exceeds the tower width
      • adding 1 cm would exceed the remaining tower capacity
      Parameters:
      i - the unique id of the lid to create and push

    • popCup

      public void popCup()
      Remove the topmost cup from the tower. Searches from the top of the stack downwards for the first cup. Changes its status (isOk = false) if no cup is found in the tower

    • popLid

      public void popLid()
      Remove the topmost lid from the tower. Searches from the top of the stack downwards for the first lid.

      Fails (isOk = false) if no lid is found in the tower.

    • removeCup

      public void removeCup(int i)
      Remove a cup with the given id from the tower. Searches through all items (base to top) to find the matching cup.

      Fails (isOk = false) if no cup with the given id is found.

      Parameters:
      i - the id of the cup to remove

    • removeLid

      public void removeLid(int i)
      Remove a lid with the given id from the tower. Searches through all items (base to top) to find the matching lid.

      Fails (isOk = false) if no lid with the given id is found.

      Parameters:
      i - the id of the lid to remove

    • orderTower

      public void orderTower()
      Order the tower from largest to smallest id (largest at bottom, smallest at top). If a cup and its matching lid (same id) are both present, the lid is placed directly on top of its cup. Remaining lids without a matching cup are placed at the top. Only items that fit within maxHeight are included; items that would cause overflow are excluded.

    • reverseTower

      public void reverseTower()
      Reverse the tower order (smallest id at bottom, largest at top). If a cup and its matching lid (same id) are both present, the lid is placed directly on top of its cup. Remaining lids without a matching cup are placed at the top. Only items that fit within maxHeight are included; items that would cause overflow are excluded.

    • height

      public int height()
      Get the current total height of all stacked items in the tower.
      Returns:
      the height in cm

    • lidedCups

      public int[] lidedCups()
      Get the ids of cups that are covered by their matching lid. A cup is considered "lided" when the item directly above it is a lid with the same id. Results are sorted from smallest to largest id.
      Returns:
      array of ids for all lided cups, sorted ascending

    • stackingItems

      public String[] stackingItems()
      Get a description of all stacked items from base to top. Each item contributes two consecutive strings: its type ("cup" or "lid") and its id. For example: ["cup","4","lid","4","cup","1"].
      Returns:
      array of strings alternating type and id from base to top

    • makeVisible

      public void makeVisible()
      Make the tower visible on the canvas. First ensures the Canvas singleton exists, then checks if the tower fits on screen. If it does not fit, shows a JOptionPane error and does not make the tower visible.

    • makeInvisible

      public void makeInvisible()
      Make the tower invisible. Erases all visual elements from the canvas but keeps the tower data intact.

    • exit

      public void exit()
      Exit the simulator. Erases the tower from the canvas and sets visibility to false.

    • ok

      public boolean ok()
      Check if the last operation was successful.
      Returns:
      true if the last operation succeeded, false otherwise

    • getWidth

      public int getWidth()
      Get the logical width of the tower.
      Returns:
      the width (maximum cup diameter that fits)

    • getMaxHeight

      public int getMaxHeight()
      Get the maximum height of the tower.
      Returns:
      the max height in cm

    • getItems

      public ArrayList<Item> getItems()
      Get the list of stacked items from base to top. Used internally by TowerGUI for drawing.
      Returns:
      the ArrayList of StackItems
